QF.id: XVI Clearing, Settlement and Risk Management_22

question.text: Devolvement margin for Options on Futures is collected during:

answer.text: The last 3 days before expiry of the option, ensuring post-devolvement futures margin obligations are met
The entire life of the option contract
Never — option buyers don't need margin

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Devolvement margin is collected in the last 3 days before Options on Futures expiry. It ensures ITM option holders can meet futures margin requirements after their options devolve into futures. **The Trap:** Not the entire life — only final 3 days. Not just expiry day. While buyers normally don't need margin, devolvement margin is an exception. **Cross-Exam Context:** Devolvement margin is specific to commodity options. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** If you hold an ITM call option nearing expiry, expect devolvement margin collection — this increases your capital requirement significantly in the last few days.
